ramp.definition: Increases mana development beyond the normal one-land-per-turn baseline over multiple turns.
must_have: Creates lasting mana acceleration or increases future mana access
Improves mana development beyond the current turn only
must_not_have: One-shot mana bursts only
Pure cost reduction
Card selection that merely finds lands to hand
Normal land drops without acceleration
ramp.edge_rule: Use for long-term acceleration. Prefer a more specific tag such as mana_dork, mana_rock, land_ramp, extra_land_drop, or untap when applicable.
ramp.priority: Do not tag ramp when a more specific mana acceleration tag fully captures the card's role.

mana_fixing.definition: Improves access to colors you may not otherwise reliably produce.
must_have: Explicitly produces multiple colors, chosen colors, any color, or searches for color-enabling lands
Directly improves color access rather than only raw mana quantity
must_not_have: Colorless-only acceleration
Cards that only increase mana quantity without fixing
Cards whose fixing is incidental and not a deckbuilding reason
mana_fixing.edge_rule: A card can be both acceleration and fixing if it clearly does both.
mana_fixing.priority: Can coexist with mana_dork, mana_rock, land_ramp, mana_filtering, or treasure.

mana_filtering.definition: Changes available mana from one color, type, or source into a different mix without necessarily increasing total mana.
must_have: Explicitly converts mana into different colors, combinations, or types
Primary function is changing mana composition rather than increasing mana quantity
must_not_have: Cards that simply produce any color with no conversion step
Pure mana acceleration with no filtering role
Card selection or land tutoring that only improves future color access
mana_filtering.edge_rule: Use filtering for cards that transform mana already available. A card that both fixes and filters may receive both tags.
mana_filtering.priority: Prefer mana_filtering over mana_fixing when the card's main role is converting mana rather than broadening source access.

mana_dork.definition: Creature with a repeatable mana-producing ability, usually activated.
must_have: Creature type line
Repeatable ability that produces mana
must_not_have: One-shot ETB mana generation
Attack-triggered Treasure or temporary mana effects
Noncreature mana sources
mana_dork.edge_rule: Do not assign to creatures that only conditionally create mana once or through a death or ETB trigger.
mana_dork.priority: Prefer mana_dork over generic ramp when the card is specifically a creature mana source.

mana_rock.definition: Artifact whose own ability produces mana.
must_have: Artifact type line
Explicit mana-producing ability on the artifact itself
must_not_have: Artifact creatures that are better classified as mana_dork if the mana role is creature-centric
Treasures unless the card's role is specifically treasure support rather than being a mana rock itself
Artifacts that only reduce costs or filter cards without producing mana
mana_rock.edge_rule: Mana rocks that also fix colors may receive mana_fixing.
mana_rock.priority: Prefer mana_rock over generic ramp when the artifact itself is the mana engine.

land_ramp.definition: Puts extra lands onto the battlefield beyond the normal land drop.
must_have: Explicitly puts lands from library, hand, graveyard, or another zone onto the battlefield
Increases future land count
must_not_have: Searching lands to hand only
Allowing extra land plays without putting lands onto the battlefield directly
Untapping lands without increasing land count
land_ramp.edge_rule: Cards that both search to hand and then place onto the battlefield qualify only if the battlefield placement is explicit.
land_ramp.priority: Prefer land_ramp over generic ramp when the acceleration is specifically extra lands entering.

ritual.definition: Produces a short-term burst of mana that does not itself create lasting mana development.
must_have: Generates extra mana for the current turn or immediate sequence only
Mana gain is one-shot rather than repeatable board-based acceleration
must_not_have: Repeatable mana abilities
Permanent extra lands or ongoing acceleration
Pure cost reduction
ritual.edge_rule: Creatures or permanents with one-time ETB mana can still be ritual if the role is clearly immediate burst mana.
ritual.priority: Do not also tag ramp unless the card separately creates lasting acceleration.

treasure.definition: Creates, consumes, scales with, or explicitly rewards Treasure tokens.
must_have: Explicit mention of Treasure
Creates Treasure, uses Treasure, or benefits from Treasure as a strategy
must_not_have: Generic artifact-token references that do not mention Treasure
Mana generation with no Treasure connection
treasure.edge_rule: A card that only creates a single incidental Treasure should still qualify if Treasure is explicit and deck-relevant.
treasure.priority: Can coexist with ramp, ritual, mana_fixing, artifacts_matter, or payoff tags.

cost_reduction.definition: Reduces the mana or activation costs of your own spells or abilities.
must_have: Explicitly makes your spells or abilities cost less
Reduction applies to your own resources
must_not_have: Opponent taxes or payment requirements
Cheating spells into play without paying rather than reducing costs
Mana generation instead of cost reduction
cost_reduction.edge_rule: Alternative costs and cost bypass effects are not cost_reduction unless the card explicitly says costs less or equivalent.
cost_reduction.priority: Use tax for opponent-facing cost increases.

cost_cheat.definition: Lets you cast, copy, put onto the battlefield, or otherwise use cards for less than their normal resource cost without directly saying they cost less.
must_have: Bypasses, replaces, or ignores normal mana payment in a meaningful way
Creates access to spells or permanents ahead of normal rate
must_not_have: Straightforward cost reduction
Ordinary reanimation if your taxonomy treats it under graveyard only and not mana systems
Mana generation that still requires normal casting costs
cost_cheat.edge_rule: Use for effects like casting without paying mana cost, cascade-style access, Sneak Attack-style deployment, or comparable rate-breaking effects.
cost_cheat.priority: Distinct from cost_reduction. Can coexist with archetype tags such as reanimator, cheat, or spells_matter.

extra_land_drop.definition: Allows you to play additional lands each turn.
must_have: Explicitly grants one or more additional land plays
must_not_have: Putting lands directly onto the battlefield
Returning lands to hand without extra land-play permission
Land search to hand only
extra_land_drop.edge_rule: Can coexist with lands_matter or landfall support, but is distinct from land_ramp.
extra_land_drop.priority: Prefer extra_land_drop over ramp when the card only changes the land-play rule.

untap.definition: Untaps permanents to create extra use, mana, or repeated activation value.
must_have: Explicit untap effect on permanents
Untap creates meaningful extra use of mana sources, activated abilities, combat utility, or engine pieces
must_not_have: Normal untap step text or reminder text
Pure vigilance
Cards that only enter untapped
untap.edge_rule: Tag when untapping is a real gameplay incentive or build-around use case, especially with lands, mana creatures, mana rocks, or repeatable engines. Omit trivial untaps that are unlikely to matter strategically.
untap.priority: Can coexist with ramp if untap effectively increases usable mana development.

mana_sink.definition: Provides repeatable or scalable use for excess mana.
must_have: Repeatable activated ability or scalable effect that meaningfully converts extra mana into value, board impact, or damage
must_not_have: Single expensive activated ability with no repeatable sink role
Triggered abilities with no mana input
Large mana costs that do not convert excess mana repeatedly or scalably
mana_sink.edge_rule: X-spells, repeatable pumps, and repeatable token-makers often qualify; ordinary activated abilities usually do not unless excess mana is a real deckbuilding use case.
mana_sink.priority: Use only when spending extra mana is a meaningful role of the card, not just possible.

conditional_mana.definition: Generates mana based on game actions such as attacking, dealing damage, or other conditions, typically in a repeatable way.
must_have: Mana generation tied to a condition or trigger (e.g., attack, damage, phase)
Repeatable through normal gameplay actions
must_not_have: One-shot spell-based mana generation (rituals)
Permanent mana sources like lands or mana rocks
conditional_mana.edge_rule: Includes combat-based mana, damage-based mana, firebending, and other triggered mana generation tied to gameplay events.
conditional_mana.priority: Prefer over ritual when mana is tied to repeatable conditions or board presence.

card_draw.definition: Directly draws one or more cards.
must_have: Explicit 'draw' instruction in oracle text
must_not_have: Look, reveal, surveil, mill, or tutor effects without drawing
Impulse-style exile play effects with no draw
Replacement or cost text that does not result in drawing
card_draw.edge_rule: Even one card drawn qualifies, but incidental minor draw may be omitted if not deck-relevant.
card_draw.priority: Can coexist with cantrip, draw_engine, loot, rummage, wheel, or card_advantage where appropriate.

draw_engine.definition: Provides repeatable or ongoing card draw over time.
must_have: Repeatable draw trigger, activated draw ability, or persistent source of ongoing draws
must_not_have: Single one-shot draw spell only
Cards that merely replace themselves once
Selection effects without actual drawing
draw_engine.edge_rule: The draw must be a meaningful recurring role, not a rare corner-case trigger.
draw_engine.priority: Prefer draw_engine over plain card_draw when the card's main role is ongoing draw; both may coexist.

card_selection.definition: Improves draw quality or choice without necessarily increasing total cards drawn.
must_have: Scry, surveil, look-at-and-choose, topdeck manipulation, hand filtering, or similar quality-improving selection
must_not_have: Pure card draw with no choice element
Tutor effects that directly search the library for a chosen card
Mill-only effects with no hand or draw quality impact
Impulse-style exile play effects better captured by impulse_draw
card_selection.edge_rule: Selection that also draws may receive both card_selection and card_draw if both are strategically meaningful.
card_selection.priority: Use tutor instead when the card searches the library for a specific card. Use impulse_draw instead when access is created through temporary exile-to-play.

cantrip.definition: Low-opportunity-cost spell or effect that replaces itself by drawing a card.
must_have: Draws a card
Has low deckbuilding cost or small primary effect such that self-replacement is central
must_not_have: Expensive draw spells whose main role is card draw volume
Repeatable engines
Effects that do not directly replace themselves
cantrip.edge_rule: Not every one-for-one draw effect is a cantrip; use only when self-replacement is a defining role.
cantrip.priority: Can coexist with card_draw, but prefer cantrip only for low-cost self-replacing effects.

loot.definition: Draws then discards, exchanging cards in hand with new information first.
must_have: Explicit draw-then-discard pattern
must_not_have: Discard-then-draw patterns
Pure card draw
Pure discard
Mill or surveil with no hand exchange
loot.edge_rule: Use even when attached to an activated ability if the exchange is deck-relevant.
loot.priority: Can coexist with card_selection; do not use for wheel effects that replace entire hands.

rummage.definition: Discards then draws, exchanging cards in hand with the discard happening first.
must_have: Explicit discard-then-draw pattern
must_not_have: Draw-then-discard patterns
Pure card draw
Pure discard
Mill or surveil with no hand exchange
rummage.edge_rule: Use when the discard is a real cost or sequencing constraint, including spells and abilities that require discard before the new card is seen.
rummage.priority: Can coexist with card_selection; do not use for wheel effects that replace entire hands.

wheel.definition: Causes one or more players to discard, shuffle away, or otherwise move away most or all of their hand and receive a new hand or comparable full refresh.
must_have: Mass hand reset or near-full hand replacement effect affecting one or more players
must_not_have: Single-card loot or rummage effects
Small hand filtering
One-for-one discard plus draw
wheel.edge_rule: Use for full or near-full hand reset patterns, including self-only versions if the effect functionally refreshes the hand rather than merely filtering it.
wheel.priority: Do not use loot or rummage when wheel more accurately captures the effect.

impulse_draw.definition: Temporarily accesses cards by exiling, revealing, or setting them aside to be played or cast for a limited time, usually this turn or until a stated duration.
must_have: Creates temporary card access without drawing into hand
Card access is granted through exile, reveal-and-play, or similar temporary permission
must_not_have: Actual drawing into hand
Library search for chosen cards
Simple look-at effects with no permission to play or cast
impulse_draw.edge_rule: Use for red-style exile play, adventure-adjacent access patterns, and similar temporary access effects when the key gameplay role is using cards from temporary access rather than drawing them.
impulse_draw.priority: Distinct from card_draw and card_selection. Can coexist with card_advantage when the effect meaningfully generates extra usable cards.

tutor.definition: Searches the library for specific cards or card classes for retrieval, reveal, cast, or placement.
must_have: Explicit library search for one or more cards by choice or criteria
must_not_have: Topdeck look-and-choose without search
Random reveal or impulse exile
Card selection from already revealed cards only
tutor.edge_rule: Land-search cards can be tutor if the search is strategically for lands, though they may also be mana_fixing or land_ramp depending on effect.
tutor.priority: Prefer tutor over card_selection whenever library search is explicit.

card_advantage.definition: Produces clear net material gain beyond one-for-one exchange, usually immediately or in a self-contained way.
must_have: Explicit multi-card gain, extra bodies, recursion, or built-in two-for-one pattern
The material gain comes from this card directly, not merely over time if unanswered
must_not_have: Pure selection without net gain
Simple one-for-one interaction
Cards that only become advantageous after repeated future turns
card_advantage.edge_rule: Use for self-contained two-for-ones and immediate material gain. Do not use just because a card is efficient or strong.
card_advantage.priority: Prefer draw_engine or value_engine when the main role is recurring advantage over time rather than immediate net gain.

value_engine.definition: Provides recurring non-draw value over time through repeated triggers, activations, or persistent board presence.
must_have: Persistent source of recurring value across multiple turns or activations
Advantage is not primarily immediate one-shot material gain
must_not_have: One-shot ETB value only
Single-use material gain
Cards whose recurring value is primarily drawing cards
value_engine.edge_rule: Use only when the card functions as an engine that keeps generating value if it remains active.
value_engine.priority: Distinct from card_advantage. Use draw_engine when the recurring value is mainly card draw.

spot_removal.definition: Removes, destroys, exiles, neutralizes, or cleanly answers a single opposing permanent.
must_have: Direct one-target answer to a permanent or creature-sized threat
must_not_have: Board wipes
Temporary tap effects only
Combat-only shrink effects that do not function as real removal
Countering spells on the stack
spot_removal.edge_rule: Pacifism-style permanent neutralization counts if it meaningfully answers a single permanent. Targeted -X/-X, damage, or stat reduction qualifies when it functionally kills or neutralizes the target as a real answer.
spot_removal.priority: Use bounce, edict, fight, or counterspell when those more specific answer modes are the primary function.

board_wipe.definition: Removes, destroys, exiles, sweeps, or broadly resets multiple permanents at once.
must_have: Affects multiple permanents or all permanents of a class in one effect
must_not_have: One-for-one removal
Combat tricks or spot shrink
Symmetrical sacrifice of one permanent each only
board_wipe.edge_rule: Mass bounce, mass exile, or broad reset effects can qualify if they function as a sweeping answer.
board_wipe.priority: Do not also tag spot_removal unless the card separately contains a distinct one-target mode that matters.

counterspell.definition: Counters a spell or ability on the stack.
must_have: Explicitly uses 'counter target spell', 'counter target ability', or equivalent stack interaction
must_not_have: Tax-only unless failure to pay explicitly counters
Ward-like protection
Removal after resolution
counterspell.edge_rule: Countering activated or triggered abilities can still qualify if the card's interaction role is stack denial.
counterspell.priority: Use even on modal cards if countering is a real deckbuilding mode.

protection.definition: Prevents removal, damage, targeting, destruction, or other hostile interaction aimed at your permanents or you.
must_have: Explicit shielding such as hexproof, indestructible, protection, phasing, regeneration, damage prevention, or save effect
must_not_have: Pure toughness boosts with no real safeguarding role
Ward on opposing cards
General evasiveness without defensive shielding
protection.edge_rule: Temporary protection spells and static protection-granting effects both qualify if their role is defensive preservation.
protection.priority: Use protection_enabler when the card mainly grants safety to other permanents rather than simply having it.

bounce.definition: Returns one or more permanents to hand.
must_have: Explicitly returns a permanent to its owner's hand
must_not_have: Blink effects returning to battlefield
Top-of-library removal
Tap effects with no zone change
bounce.edge_rule: Temporary bounce attached to ETB, spell resolution, or activated ability all qualifies if bounce is meaningful. Land bounce can qualify here when the effect is primarily bounce rather than mana denial.
bounce.priority: Use bounce instead of spot_removal when the answer mode is specifically returning to hand.

fight.definition: Uses fight, bite, or creature-damage-based mechanics as removal.
must_have: Explicit fight wording or creature dealing damage to another creature as the removal mechanism
must_not_have: Ordinary combat
Noncreature direct damage spells
Stat boosts without creature-damage resolution
fight.edge_rule: Cards that cause only one-sided creature damage still qualify if creature-based removal is the main interaction mode.
fight.priority: Prefer fight over spot_removal when the card's answer mode is specifically creature-based fighting or biting.

edict.definition: Forces an opponent to sacrifice a permanent as the primary answer mode.
must_have: Explicitly causes an opponent to sacrifice a permanent
must_not_have: Self-sacrifice costs
Symmetrical sacrifice for all players unless the main role is still forcing opponent sacrifice
Destroy or exile effects
edict.edge_rule: Use only when forced sacrifice is the interaction mode, not when sacrifice is merely an optional rider.
edict.priority: Prefer edict over spot_removal when sacrifice is the central text.

graveyard_hate.definition: Disrupts opposing graveyards, exiles graveyard cards, prevents graveyard use, or punishes graveyard strategies.
must_have: Explicit graveyard disruption, prevention, exiling, replacement, or shutdown aimed at opposing or shared graveyard use
must_not_have: Using your own graveyard positively
Self-mill without disruption
Incidental anti-recursion text that is too minor to matter
graveyard_hate.edge_rule: Cards that repeatedly or strongly constrain graveyard usage are especially clear fits. Symmetrical graveyard shutdown still qualifies when its strategic role is to suppress graveyard decks.
graveyard_hate.priority: Can coexist with control_piece, stax, or lock_piece if the disruption is ongoing.

hand_disruption.definition: Reveals, removes, or otherwise disrupts cards in an opponent's hand.
must_have: Explicit hand reveal, discard, exile-from-hand, or selection from opponent's hand
must_not_have: Wheel effects that are broad hand reset rather than targeted disruption
Discarding your own hand
Tax or counter effects with no hand interaction
hand_disruption.edge_rule: Targeted and mass discard both qualify if disrupting the opponent's hand is a real role.
hand_disruption.priority: Can coexist with control_piece or cheap_interaction.

land_destruction.definition: Destroys, exiles, sacrifices, or otherwise removes lands from the battlefield.
must_have: Explicitly answers lands already on the battlefield
must_not_have: Preventing land untaps only
Searching lands from library
Bouncing nonlands only
land_destruction.edge_rule: Land bounce can qualify only if land denial is a central role and the target can be lands.
land_destruction.priority: Can coexist with mana_denial when both land removal and broader resource denial are present.

mana_denial.definition: Restricts opposing mana production, land use, untaps, or resource development without necessarily destroying lands.
must_have: Explicitly limits lands, mana usage, untapping, or mana production
must_not_have: Simple cost increase only if tax is the more precise tag
Pure land destruction with no ongoing denial aspect
General control cards with no mana or resource constraint
mana_denial.edge_rule: Use for resource denial patterns like tapping down lands, preventing untaps, restricting mana generation, or denying usable mana without directly increasing spell costs.
mana_denial.priority: Prefer tax for payment-based cost increases; prefer land_destruction when the main role is removing lands.

cheap_interaction.definition: Provides efficient low-cost disruption relative to its effect.
must_have: Mana value is low enough that cheapness is a real deckbuilding feature
Card is interaction first, not just proactive utility
must_not_have: Expensive answers
Cards whose low cost is not strategically central
Noninteractive spells
cheap_interaction.edge_rule: Use sparingly; the cheap rate must be a meaningful reason the card is played.
cheap_interaction.priority: Secondary tag only. Pair with a specific interaction tag such as spot_removal, counterspell, or hand_disruption.

self_mill.definition: Puts cards from your own library into your own graveyard.
must_have: Explicitly moves cards from your library to your graveyard
must_not_have: Discard from hand only
Opponent mill only
Tutor or surveil that does not actually place cards into the graveyard
self_mill.edge_rule: Use even for small amounts if self-mill is strategically relevant.
self_mill.priority: Can coexist with graveyard_enabler or graveyard_payoff.

mill.definition: Puts cards from an opponent's library into their graveyard.
must_have: Explicitly causes an opponent to put cards from library into graveyard
must_not_have: Self-mill only
Discard from hand
Exile from library without graveyard placement
mill.edge_rule: Use when the effect materially contributes to a milling plan, not for trivial incidental one-card cases unless still deck-relevant.
mill.priority: Do not use for self-targeting graveyard setup.

recursion.definition: Returns cards from graveyard to hand, library, battlefield, or replay permission, excluding creature-to-battlefield reanimation when that more specific tag applies.
must_have: Explicitly returns cards from graveyard or allows reuse from graveyard
must_not_have: Pure graveyard filling
Creature-only battlefield return when reanimation is the correct specific tag
Spell-only return when spell_recursion is the more precise tag
Land-only return when land_recursion is the more precise tag
recursion.edge_rule: Use recursion for broad or mixed graveyard return effects that are not better captured by a more specific graveyard tag.
recursion.priority: Prefer reanimation, spell_recursion, or land_recursion when those fit exactly.

reanimation.definition: Returns creature cards from graveyard directly to the battlefield.
must_have: Explicitly puts creature card(s) from a graveyard onto the battlefield
must_not_have: Returning creatures to hand
Returning noncreatures only
Casting from graveyard instead of directly returning to battlefield
reanimation.edge_rule: Can include your graveyard or any graveyard, but the creature-to-battlefield pattern must be explicit.
reanimation.priority: Prefer reanimation over recursion whenever creature-to-battlefield is the core effect.

spell_recursion.definition: Returns instants or sorceries from graveyard for reuse or allows them to be recast.
must_have: Explicitly returns, copies from, or grants reuse/cast permission for instants or sorceries in graveyards
must_not_have: Creature recursion
General recursion with no spell specificity unless the spell role is not central
Card draw that incidentally finds spells
spell_recursion.edge_rule: Flashback-like or cast-from-graveyard support can qualify if spell reuse is explicit and meaningful.
spell_recursion.priority: Prefer spell_recursion over generic recursion when the target class is instants and sorceries.

land_recursion.definition: Returns lands from graveyard, replays lands from graveyard, or otherwise reuses lands specifically from graveyard.
must_have: Explicit land-specific graveyard return or replay permission
must_not_have: General recursion with no land specificity unless land reuse is the primary role
Land search from library
Extra land drop with no graveyard component
land_recursion.edge_rule: Includes effects letting you play lands from graveyard.
land_recursion.priority: Prefer land_recursion over generic recursion when the graveyard target is lands.

graveyard_enabler.definition: Actively fills, stocks, discards to, or otherwise sets up graveyards to support other effects.
must_have: Explicit action that loads cards into a graveyard or uses the graveyard as setup
must_not_have: Payoff cards that merely care about graveyards being full
Recursion with no setup role
Incidental discard or mill too minor to matter
graveyard_enabler.edge_rule: Self-mill, rummage-for-yard value, and discard outlets often qualify when they function as setup tools.
graveyard_enabler.priority: Use enabler for setup; use graveyard_payoff for reward.

graveyard_payoff.definition: Gets stronger, cheaper, larger, or otherwise more valuable because cards are in graveyards.
must_have: Explicit scaling, cost reduction, advantage, or reward tied to graveyard contents or graveyard use
must_not_have: Cards that merely fill the graveyard
Straight recursion spells with no payoff scaling
Cards that use graveyard only incidentally
graveyard_payoff.edge_rule: The card must benefit from the graveyard state, not just interact with it.
graveyard_payoff.priority: Can coexist with recursion or reanimation only if the card both rewards and uses the graveyard.

discard_outlet.definition: Provides a repeatable or intentional way to discard cards from hand, typically to enable graveyard strategies or synergies.
must_have: Explicitly allows or requires discarding cards from your hand
Discarding is a controllable or repeatable action, not just a one-time drawback
must_not_have: One-time discard as an additional cost with no synergy relevance
Opponent discard effects
Random discard with no strategic control unless still used for graveyard setup
discard_outlet.edge_rule: Looting and rummaging effects can qualify if the discard portion is strategically important for graveyard setup. Activated abilities that allow repeatable discard are strong indicators.
discard_outlet.priority: Use discard_outlet when enabling discard is a primary role. Can coexist with graveyard_enabler, rummage, loot, or madness_synergy.

token_maker.definition: Creates creature or noncreature tokens as a meaningful part of its effect.
must_have: Explicitly creates one or more tokens
must_not_have: Counters only
Copies that are not tokens unless the text creates token copies
Incidental token mention with no creation
token_maker.edge_rule: Includes creature and noncreature tokens (Treasure, Clue, Food, etc.). Single-token effects qualify if token creation is a real reason the card is included.
token_maker.priority: Can coexist with treasure, clue-like roles, sac_fodder, or token_payoff.

token_payoff.definition: Benefits from creating, controlling, sacrificing, counting, or going wide with tokens specifically.
must_have: Explicit reward for tokens as tokens, not just for creatures generally
must_not_have: Cards that only create tokens
Generic go-wide rewards that do not care whether the bodies are tokens
Sacrifice payoffs with no token-specific incentive
token_payoff.edge_rule: Use only when token-specific language or token-centric reward is explicit or central.
token_payoff.priority: Prefer go_wide when the reward is broad creature quantity rather than token specificity.

go_wide.definition: Rewards controlling or attacking with many creatures or bodies at once.
must_have: Scales with number of creatures, attackers, or bodies under your control
must_not_have: Single-creature voltron or tall strategies
Token-specific reward when token_payoff is more precise
Simple anthem with no scaling or strategic many-bodies reward if anthem is the better tag
go_wide.edge_rule: Includes attack-based scaling and combat incentives for wide boards, even without token references.
go_wide.priority: Can coexist with anthem, token_maker, or typal.

go_tall.definition: Rewards concentrating stats, counters, auras, or combat value onto one or a small number of creatures.
must_have: Effect strongly favors one creature or a very small board rather than many bodies
must_not_have: Wide team pumps
Generic counters cards that do not encourage concentration
Single-target protection that is not a real tall-strategy reward
go_tall.edge_rule: Voltron-style enhancement, one-creature scaling, and concentrated counter growth usually qualify.
go_tall.priority: Do not use with go_wide unless the card genuinely supports both plans.

plus1_plus1_counters.definition: Uses +1/+1 counters as a direct and central mechanical feature.
must_have: Explicitly places, moves, removes, or modifies +1/+1 counters
References +1/+1 counters in oracle text as a primary mechanic
must_not_have: Cards that grow without using counters
Cards referencing only other counter types
Temporary stat boosts without counters
plus1_plus1_counters.edge_rule: Do not tag if +1/+1 counters are incidental and not a core reason to include the card.
plus1_plus1_counters.priority: Represents the presence of the mechanic, not whether it is a payoff.

counters_payoff.definition: Benefits significantly from counters being placed, scaling or generating value from them.
must_have: Explicit benefit when counters are placed
Scaling based on number of counters
Converts counters into value, damage, cards, mana, or board advantage
must_not_have: Cards that only place counters on themselves
Cards that incidentally receive counters without broader synergy
Cards where counters are not tied to strategic reward
counters_payoff.edge_rule: Use only when the card is a reward for a counters strategy, not just a participant.
counters_payoff.priority: May coexist with plus1_plus1_counters only if both conditions are clearly met.

proliferate.definition: Adds to existing counters on permanents or players through proliferate or equivalent counter-expansion effects.
must_have: Explicit proliferate wording or direct addition to existing counters across multiple permanents or players
must_not_have: Simply placing new counters without growing existing ones
proliferate.edge_rule: Primarily for true proliferate or near-identical effects that expand existing counters across multiple objects.
proliferate.priority: Can coexist with plus1_plus1_counters or counters_payoff.

anthem.definition: Broadly increases the stats of multiple creatures at once.
must_have: Static or repeatable effect that increases power, toughness, or both for a broad set of creatures
must_not_have: Single-target pumps
Temporary combat tricks
Counters-only growth that is not a broad team boost
anthem.edge_rule: Team-wide temporary effects usually belong under combat_trick unless their primary role is persistent enhancement.
anthem.priority: Use lord when the boost is subtype-specific and that typal restriction is central.

lord.definition: Provides a tribal or subtype-based stat boost or similar broad enhancement to a named creature type.
must_have: Explicitly boosts or improves a named creature type or subtype group
must_not_have: Generic anthem with no type restriction
Self-only buffs
Typal references that do not actually enhance the tribe broadly
lord.edge_rule: Non-stat tribal enhancement can qualify if it functions like a classic lord for the tribe.
lord.priority: Prefer lord over anthem when the effect is subtype-based.

sacrifice_outlet.definition: Provides a repeatable or strategically central way to sacrifice your own permanents for value, enabling, or payoff.
must_have: Explicitly allows you to sacrifice your own permanents
Sacrificing is repeatable or a primary deckbuilding role of the card
must_not_have: Cards that only sacrifice themselves as a one-shot cost
Forced sacrifice from opponents only
Incidental self-sacrifice that is not a meaningful engine role
sacrifice_outlet.edge_rule: Repeatable free or low-cost sacrifice is a strong indicator. One-time sacrifice effects are not outlets unless they clearly function as enablers.
sacrifice_outlet.priority: Can coexist with sac_payoff, death_payoff, or aristocrats.

sac_fodder.definition: Provides expendable permanents that are useful to sacrifice.
must_have: Creates or supplies disposable permanents that can be profitably sacrificed
The expendable material is a meaningful part of why the card is played
must_not_have: Ordinary creatures with no expendable-material role
Cards that only sacrifice themselves
Payoffs that do not actually provide bodies or material
sac_fodder.edge_rule: Token-makers often qualify if the bodies are primarily used as sacrifice material rather than combat bodies.
sac_fodder.priority: Canonical location for sac_fodder. Can coexist with token_maker.

death_payoff.definition: Benefits when creatures or permanents die.
must_have: Explicit trigger or scaling effect tied to creatures or permanents dying
must_not_have: Cards that merely cause death without benefiting from it
Sacrifice outlets with no reward
Leave-the-battlefield triggers unrelated to death
death_payoff.edge_rule: Includes death of any permanents unless restricted by text. Use when the card gains value from death broadly, not just sacrifice.
death_payoff.priority: Prefer sac_payoff when the reward is specifically tied to sacrificing rather than dying.

must_have: Explicit trigger, scaling, or reward tied to sacrificing permanents
must_not_have: General death triggers with no sacrifice specificity
Cards that only enable sacrifice
Cards that merely require a sacrifice cost without rewarding it
sac_payoff.edge_rule: If the card rewards sacrifice directly (not just death), use sac_payoff.
sac_payoff.priority: Prefer sac_payoff over death_payoff when sacrifice is the precise condition.

lifegain_enabler.definition: Causes you to gain life as a meaningful function.
must_have: Explicit life gain for you or your controller
must_not_have: Life total setting or prevention with no gain
Cards that only care about gained life but do not gain it
Tiny incidental life gain that is not strategically relevant
lifegain_enabler.edge_rule: Lifelink qualifies when life gain is a meaningful part of the card’s role, not just incidental.
lifegain_enabler.priority: Can coexist with life_drain or lifegain_payoff if both apply.

lifegain_payoff.definition: Benefits from life being gained through triggers, scaling, or rewards.
must_have: Explicitly rewards life gain with a trigger, counters, cards, damage, tokens, or scaling
must_not_have: Cards that only gain life
Cards that merely preserve life totals
Cards where life gain is unrelated to the reward
lifegain_payoff.edge_rule: Includes cards that check for life gain events or thresholds (e.g., 'whenever you gain life' or 'if you gained life this turn').
lifegain_payoff.priority: Do not use for pure life gain sources; use lifegain_enabler there.

life_drain.definition: Causes opponents to lose life, often paired with you gaining life.
must_have: Explicit opponent life loss tied to the card
must_not_have: Pure damage-based burn with no drain identity
Life gain only
life_drain.edge_rule: Life gain is common but not required. Use when the card’s identity is incremental life swing rather than burst damage.
life_drain.priority: Can coexist with death_payoff, sac_payoff, or aristocrats.

aristocrats.definition: Archetype tag for cards that clearly belong in sacrifice/death/drain shells.
must_have: Strongly supports or rewards a sacrifice/death/value loop
Would commonly be included in an aristocrats-style deck because of that role
must_not_have: Generic sacrifice outlets with no payoff
Generic death payoffs with no sacrifice-shell relevance
Cards only loosely related to sacrificing
aristocrats.edge_rule: Use sparingly and only as a secondary archetype tag after functional tags like sac_payoff, death_payoff, sac_fodder, or sacrifice_outlet are assigned.
aristocrats.priority: Secondary archetype tag only. Never use as a substitute for functional sacrifice/death tags.

haste_enabler.definition: Grants or enables haste or immediate activation use for itself or other permanents as a meaningful role.
must_have: Explicitly grants haste or allows immediate use equivalent to haste (including activated abilities)
must_not_have: Card simply having haste with no enabling role or deckbuilding significance
Untap effects that are not functionally haste-enabling
Cards that only accelerate mana
haste_enabler.edge_rule: Includes effects that allow creatures or permanents to use tap abilities immediately.
haste_enabler.priority: Use more readily when the card grants haste to other permanents.

evasive_threat.definition: Threat with offensive evasion such as flying, menace, trample, unblockable, skulk, shadow, fear, or intimidate.
must_have: A meaningful offensive body or threat profile
Explicit offensive evasion keyword or equivalent text
must_not_have: Deathtouch, vigilance, first strike, reach, ward, or hexproof alone
Purely defensive flyers with no real pressure role
Cards that only grant evasion to others
evasive_threat.edge_rule: Use when evasion materially contributes to pressure, not just incidental keywords.
evasive_threat.priority: Use evasion_enabler when the card grants evasion to others.

evasion_enabler.definition: Grants offensive evasion to other permanents.
must_have: Explicitly grants offensive evasion keywords or equivalent to other permanents
must_not_have: Card merely having evasion itself
Protection or shielding that is not offensive evasion
Temporary combat tricks unless evasion is the main role
evasion_enabler.edge_rule: Use when enabling attacks to connect is a meaningful role.

protection_enabler.definition: Grants hexproof, indestructible, protection, ward-like safety, or similar shielding to other permanents.
must_have: Explicitly grants defensive protection or safeguarding to other permanents
must_not_have: Card merely protecting itself
Evasion-granting with no real shielding
Temporary stat boosts without true protection
protection_enabler.edge_rule: Use when protecting other permanents is a primary role.

combat_trick.definition: A temporary, combat-focused effect primarily intended to influence combat outcomes, usually at instant speed.
must_have: Temporary change to power/toughness, combat keywords, or survivability
Effect is primarily usable during combat
must_not_have: Sorcery-speed stat changes not clearly combat-focused
Permanent stat increases such as counters
Static anthem effects
Pure removal spells
High-cost spells with incidental combat relevance
combat_trick.edge_rule: If the primary purpose is not to swing combat this turn, do not tag.
combat_trick.priority: If also removal, prefer interaction tags.

blink.definition: Exiles and returns permanents to retrigger ETB abilities, reset them, or protect them.
must_have: Explicitly exiles and returns a permanent to the battlefield
must_not_have: Bounce to hand only
Phasing only
Temporary exile that does not return
blink.edge_rule: Includes both self-blink and blinking other permanents when used for value or protection.
blink.priority: Can coexist with etb or protection.

etb.definition: Has a meaningful effect that triggers when the permanent enters the battlefield.
must_have: Explicit 'enters the battlefield' trigger
must_not_have: Morph or face-up triggers
'When cast' triggers
'When dies' triggers
Attack triggers
Upkeep or end step triggers
Static abilities
etb.edge_rule: Do not tag low-impact ETBs that are not a primary reason to include the card.

etb_payoff.definition: Benefits from permanents entering the battlefield.
must_have: Explicit trigger or scaling tied to permanents entering the battlefield
must_not_have: Cards that merely have ETB effects themselves
Leave-the-battlefield or cast triggers
Static effects with no ETB reward
etb_payoff.edge_rule: Use when the card rewards ETB events broadly, not just itself.

ltb_payoff.definition: Benefits from permanents leaving the battlefield.
must_have: Explicit trigger or reward tied to permanents leaving the battlefield
must_not_have: Death-only payoffs if death_payoff is more precise
Blink cards with no reward
Sacrifice outlets with no payoff
ltb_payoff.edge_rule: Includes blink and exile-return patterns when broadly rewarded.
ltb_payoff.priority: Prefer death_payoff when limited to dying.

must_have: Explicit 'whenever this creature attacks' or similar attack trigger
must_not_have: Combat damage triggers
Static combat bonuses
Non-combat triggers
attack_trigger.edge_rule: Includes attack triggers that generate value, tokens, or effects upon declaring attacks.
attack_trigger.priority: Can coexist with threat or go_wide.

combat_damage_trigger.definition: Triggers when a creature deals combat damage to a player, creature, or permanent.
must_have: Explicit combat damage trigger condition
must_not_have: Attack triggers
Non-combat damage triggers
Static effects
combat_damage_trigger.edge_rule: Includes saboteur-style effects that generate value on hit.

threat.definition: A permanent that, if unanswered, generates significant pressure, advantage, or game progression.
must_have: Repeatable advantage, or meaningful damage pressure, or snowballing board impact
must_not_have: Low-impact creatures
Purely defensive cards
Narrow synergy pieces
Cards requiring heavy setup
threat.edge_rule: If only strong in synergy shells, prefer payoff or build-around tags.

sticky_threat.definition: A threat that is difficult to remove or continues providing value through interaction.
must_have: Qualifies as a threat
Has resilience such as recursion, replacement, protection, or persistent value
must_not_have: Easily removed threats
Defensive resilient cards with no pressure
Minor one-time protection
sticky_threat.edge_rule: Stickiness must be a defining feature.
sticky_threat.priority: Only if both threat and resilience are clear.

spellslinger.definition: Rewards casting or copying instants, sorceries, or multiple noncreature spells.
must_have: Explicit trigger, scaling, or reward tied to casting or copying instants, sorceries, or noncreature spells
must_not_have: Cards that are merely instants or sorceries
Single spell-copy effects with no ongoing spells-matter incentive
spellslinger.edge_rule: Includes magecraft-style effects and similar cast-or-copy triggers.
spellslinger.priority: Can coexist with prowess, storm, or copy_spell.

storm.definition: Rewards casting many spells in a single turn or scales with spell count in a burst-combo pattern.
must_have: Explicit storm mechanic or clear reward for casting many spells in a single turn
must_not_have: General spellslinger cards that reward spell casting over time
Single spell-copy effects with no spell-count scaling
storm.edge_rule: Reserve for burst sequencing, not general spell value.
storm.priority: Prefer storm over spellslinger when the card specifically rewards high spell count in one turn.

prowess.definition: Rewards noncreature spell casting with temporary or scaling combat/stat bonuses.
must_have: Explicit prowess or equivalent combat/stat growth from casting noncreature spells
must_not_have: General spellslinger payoffs that do not affect combat
Permanent counters unless combat growth is the central role
prowess.edge_rule: Use for combat-centric spell reward patterns.

copy_spell.definition: Copies spells on the stack or as they are cast.
must_have: Explicitly copies one or more spells
must_not_have: Copying permanents only
Recasting from graveyard without copying
copy_spell.edge_rule: Central role must involve copying spells, not incidental duplication.

cast_from_elsewhere.definition: Allows or rewards casting spells from zones other than hand (e.g., exile, graveyard, library).
must_have: Explicit permission or reward for casting spells from non-hand zones
must_not_have: Standard casting from hand
Simple recursion to hand without casting
Impulse draw without meaningful casting synergy
cast_from_elsewhere.edge_rule: Includes flashback, escape, exile-play, adventure, and similar mechanics when casting from alternate zones is central.
cast_from_elsewhere.priority: Can coexist with spellslinger, impulse_draw, or recursion.

copy_permanent.definition: Copies permanents as permanents or creates copies of permanents.
must_have: Explicitly copies a permanent or creates a copy token of a permanent
must_not_have: Copying spells only
Creature-only copying when clone is more precise
copy_permanent.edge_rule: Use when copying is not limited strictly to creatures.

must_have: Explicitly enters as or becomes a copy of a creature
must_not_have: Copying any permanent broadly
Token generation without copying
clone.edge_rule: Use whenever creature copying is the central function.
clone.priority: Prefer clone over copy_permanent when applicable.

artifacts_matter.definition: Benefits from artifacts entering, existing, being cast, or being used as a class.
must_have: Explicit reward, count, or scaling tied to artifacts
must_not_have: Being an artifact alone
artifacts_matter.edge_rule: Includes artifact tokens (Treasure, Clue, Food) when relevant to the strategy.
artifacts_matter.priority: Can coexist with treasure, equipment, or sac_payoff.

enchantments_matter.definition: Rewards, counts, or interacts with enchantments as a class.
must_have: Explicit synergy or reward tied to enchantments (including constellation-style triggers)
must_not_have: Being an enchantment only
enchantments_matter.edge_rule: Includes constellation and similar ETB enchantment triggers.

equipment.definition: Interacts with Equipment or benefits from equipping.
must_have: Explicitly references Equipment or equip mechanics
must_not_have: Generic artifact synergy
equipment.edge_rule: Use when equipment interaction is central.

aura.definition: Interacts with Auras or benefits from enchanting.
must_have: Explicitly references Auras or enchanted permanents
must_not_have: Generic enchantment synergy
aura.edge_rule: Being an Aura is not sufficient unless aura synergy is central.

lands_matter.definition: Benefits from lands entering, being played, being sacrificed, being counted, being replayed, or otherwise being used as a strategic resource.
must_have: Explicit reward, count, scaling, or synergy tied to lands
must_not_have: Cards that merely produce mana
Simple land tutors with no broader land synergy
Single land interaction that is not a lands-strategy reward
lands_matter.edge_rule: Use when the card is part of a lands shell, not merely adjacent to mana development. Includes cards that care about lands entering, leaving, being in graveyards, or being replayed.
lands_matter.priority: Can coexist with landfall, land_recursion, extra_land_drop, or sac_land.

landfall.definition: Triggers when lands enter the battlefield under your control or in an equivalent land-entry trigger pattern.
must_have: Explicit trigger tied to lands entering the battlefield
must_not_have: Playing lands with no trigger
Extra land plays with no reward
Land-count scaling with no ETB trigger
landfall.edge_rule: Use for explicit land-entry-trigger rewards; do not use for broader lands-matter cards without that pattern.

utility_land.definition: Land whose primary value includes a meaningful non-mana ability beyond simple fixing or basic mana production.
must_have: Land type line
Meaningful non-mana activated, triggered, or static ability
must_not_have: Basic lands
Simple dual, fetch, or fixing lands with no meaningful utility beyond mana access
Lands whose only text is entering tapped or producing mana variants
utility_land.edge_rule: The non-mana ability must be a real reason to include the land, not trivial text.
utility_land.priority: Can coexist with manland, sac_land, land_destruction, lands_matter, or recursion-related tags if applicable.

manland.definition: Land that can become a creature or otherwise function as an attacking or blocking creature.
must_have: Land type line
Explicit ability to become a creature, animate itself, or enter as a creature land
must_not_have: Lands that only create creature tokens
Utility lands with no self-animation
Nonland permanents that animate lands temporarily without the land itself carrying that role
manland.edge_rule: Use when creature-land functionality is a meaningful role, whether as threat, blocker, or resilient win condition.
manland.priority: Prefer manland over generic utility_land when self-animation is a central reason the land is played, though both may coexist.

sac_land.definition: Land that sacrifices itself or is commonly used by sacrificing lands as a central part of its function.
must_have: Land type line
Explicit sacrifice of itself or another land as a meaningful cost, effect, or strategic function
must_not_have: Lands that merely go to graveyard through normal gameplay
Lands with no sacrifice text or sacrifice role
Nonland cards that sacrifice lands
sac_land.edge_rule: Includes fetch-style self-sacrificing lands, self-sacrifice utility lands, and lands whose central role involves sacrificing lands for value, fixing, denial, or graveyard synergy.
sac_land.priority: Can coexist with mana_fixing, land_destruction, lands_matter, or land_recursion.

typal.definition: Rewards, references, counts, or meaningfully cares about creature types or a named subtype.
must_have: Explicit subtype or creature-type reference that matters strategically
must_not_have: Having a creature type line only
Lord effects can still be typal, but do not use typal for cards with no actual tribal/textual payoff
Flavor references with no rules meaning
typal.edge_rule: Use when the card belongs in a tribal/typal shell because of subtype-based synergy or reward.
typal.priority: Can coexist with lord, payoff, or build_around.
